data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T050802-370f3dc4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T050802-370f3dc4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T050802-370f3dc4/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T050802-370f3dc4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T050802-370f3dc4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-64c24626">
    <title>Delete legacy direct agent execution transcript handling</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
DDx still contains a legacy direct Claude stream-json execution/transcript path. After DDx runs agent work through Fizeau, this alternate code path violates the boundary and can produce divergent transcript semantics from Fizeau.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go contains runClaudeStreamingFn, parseClaudeStream, claudeStreamArgsUnsupported, and Fizeau-style event synthesis from Claude stream-json.
- cli/internal/agent/runner.go and tests still allow DDX_USE_NEW_AGENT_PATH / legacy fallback routing to select DDx-owned parser behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
Delete the DDx-owned Claude stream parser and any execution fallback that can route transcript-producing agent work through it. DDx should invoke Fizeau for agent execution and consume Fizeau events/results through the opaque/final-result adapter.

NON-SCOPE
- DDx final Result projection from Fizeau final events.
- Fizeau harness internals.
- Historical artifact readability for already-created logs.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Production code has no DDx-owned Claude stream-json parser; rg -n 'runClaudeStreamingFn|parseClaudeStream|claudeStreamArgsUnsupported|DDX_USE_NEW_AGENT_PATH' cli/internal/agent cli/cmd returns no production hits.
2. Production code no longer synthesizes Fizeau/internal transcript events such as tool.call/tool.result from Claude stream-json.
3. Tests that covered the deleted parser are removed or rewritten around the Fizeau service adapter boundary.
4. TestAgentExecution_UsesFizeauServicePathOnly or equivalent proves transcript-producing agent work cannot select the deleted direct parser.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'Test.*Claude|Test.*Agent|Test.*Service|Test.*Fizeau' -count=1 passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:harnesses, kind:cleanup, upstream-fizeau</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-dda48755" spec-id="FEAT-006" base-rev="089128a015670215a3158d9c57f4844f0b0538ea" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T050802-370f3dc4"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-006" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md">Feature: DDx Agent Service (consumer of ddx-agent contract)</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `bf8261c` with subject `refactor(agent): remove legacy claude stream parser [ddx-64c24626]`.

- Removed the direct Claude stream-json execution branch from [`cli/internal/agent/runner.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-64c24626-20260506T050802-370f3dc4/cli/internal/agent/runner.go:191), so that path now just uses the normal executor flow.
- Simplified [`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-64c24626-20260506T050802-370f3dc4/cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:3) to always delegate to the Fizeau service path.
- Deleted the legacy parser and its tests: [`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-64c24626-20260506T050802-370f3dc4/cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go) and [`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-64c24626-20260506T050802-370f3dc4/cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go).
- Added [`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-64c24626-20260506T050802-370f3dc4/cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service_test.go:125) to prove transcript-producing agent execution goes through the service adapter boundary.

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'Test.*Claude|Test.*Agent|Test.*Service|Test.*Fizeau' -count=1`
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -count=1`
- `env CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit`

The bead-scoped symbol grep is clean: no `runClaudeStreamingFn`, `parseClaudeStream`, `claudeStreamArgsUnsupported`, or `DDX_USE_NEW_AGENT_PATH` remain under `cli/internal/agent` or `cli/cmd`.
